communicationJob: By the end, a technical reviewer should understand what NodeKit now proves, which real product slices support the claim, and which artifact and publication gates remain open.

slides.job: Open with the system's concrete promise.
slides.body: One manifest connects runtime, evaluation, browser QA, and proof.
slides.dominantVisual: One clean lifecycle from brief to public proof with receipt checkpoints.

slides.job: Show why code generation alone is insufficient.
slides.takeaway: The hard part is keeping every proof gate on one execution path.
slides.body: Parity breaks when benchmark, browser, deployment, and public claims follow different implementations.
bullets: One implementation must serve every gate
slides.dominantVisual: Fragmented parallel paths collapsing into one compiled definition.

slides.job: Prove the factory rather than describe it.
slides.audienceQuestion: Does the empty-directory path actually work?
slides.takeaway: Two presets passed the full factory path in 37.221 seconds.
slides.body: From empty directories, NodeKit installed, compiled, ran, evaluated, benchmarked, and proof-checked both presets.
slides.dominantVisual: Two receipt cards joined to one factory-acceptance digest.

slides.audienceQuestion: Can the system survive a real agentic product journey?
slides.takeaway: The Casca lab passed every local gate without making a credit decision.
slides.body: On commit 6c8d7d9, the synthetic lab passed deterministic, live-provider, browser, reload, export, and identity gates.
slides.dominantVisual: Product screenshot annotated with graph query, proposal, and receipt checkpoints.

formula.description: Synthetic local workflow only; no automated credit decision.

slides.job: Explain the research and learning boundary.
slides.audienceQuestion: How does NodeKit approach Agentic RL responsibly?
slides.takeaway: Protect the scorer first; train only after its boundaries are credible.
slides.body: The current baseline is behavior cloning, not RL. It passed protected local evaluation; the strict Azure-only live route failed closed with no fallback.
slides.dominantVisual: Trajectory to protected reward to shadow candidate flow, with behavior cloning labeled as the current stage.

slides.job: Introduce Founder Quest as the connected product surface.
slides.audienceQuestion: What does the graph make possible for a user?
slides.takeaway: Founder Quest passed all 15 hosted checks on one exact production revision.
slides.body: Read-only Founder Quest connects quests, a bounded graph, sourced answers, authority boundaries, and proof in four isolated browser contexts.
slides.dominantVisual: Verified production Founder Quest view with quest rail, bounded graph, answer panel, and proof strip.

metric.label: hosted checks passed; synthetic and read-only

slides.job: Connect the external rare-disease grant scenario.
slides.audienceQuestion: How would a researcher use this today?
slides.takeaway: A real grant scenario tests the graph without claiming biological proof.
slides.body: Anthropic's rare-disease grant is an independent forcing function for mapping research, novelty, evaluation, authority, and readiness, not an affiliation.
bullets: Research synthesis does not prove biology

formula.description: Research synthesis informs an application; it does not prove biology.

slides.job: Explain the graph of loops and close with a falsifiable invitation.
slides.audienceQuestion: How do the pieces improve, and what should reviewers do next?
slides.takeaway: Loops need external anchors. Bring one workflow and inspect the proof.
slides.body: Research, build, evaluation, proof, presentation, and feedback can watch one another. Only external anchors close the graph.
bullets: Inspect pass, fail, and human-review status
slides.dominantVisual: Loop graph with external reality anchors and three reviewer entry points.

formula.description: External sources, commits, artifacts, and observed outcomes close the loop.
